signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 163.16, marking a 40-year high as Middle East tensions and rising crude oil prices ($85.7) bolster the dollar's yield-backed appeal. The 275bp Fed-BoJ carry differential (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%) remains the primary structural driver, while the 25d risk reversal at -1.42 shows a moderate put skew as the options market pays for protection against a potential BoJ intervention. JPY COT LF net short -105,238 confirms the carry trade is heavily crowded, and the pair remains technically overextended above its SMA20/50/200 cluster. Despite the geopolitical risk, the carry advantage dominates until VIX clears 25 and triggers a true safe-haven rotation. Risk: BoJ intervenes or VIX breaks above 25, forcing a disorderly unwind of the crowded JPY short.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Policy + Volatility + CESI
signals.text: EUR/USD holds at 1.1405, just above its SMA20 of 1.1416, as markets price a hawkish-leaning hold for the July 23 ECB meeting. While the ECB (2.25%) is in a hiking cycle vs the Fed (3.75%) on hold, the 150bp carry advantage still favors the USD. Options pricing shows an elevated vol premium with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.7%, indicating the market is paying for protection ahead of the rate decision. This caution is supported by a strong negative CESI score for the Euro (-17 net misses), suggesting recent data has failed to support the hawkish narrative. The pair remains capped by the SMA50 at 1.1526, reflecting structural downward pressure. ECB holds at 2.25%.

signals.title: NZD/JPY — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail
signals.text: NZD/JPY trades at 95.12 (derived) with a significant divergence between speculative positioning and central bank bias. COT LF net short -29,582 shows specs are heavily fading the NZD, despite the RBNZ (2.5%) maintaining a hiking stance and a positive real rate differential over the BoJ. Retail sentiment is at an extreme 85% short, creating a high-conviction contrarian setup for a move higher if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise on September 2. The pair is technically supported by the NZD SMA20/50/200 cluster, which currently sits below the spot price. A break higher would likely force a rapid cover of the crowded institutional short. Risk: RBNZ shifts to a neutral bias, validating the current spec short position.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Carry + Volatility + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD is holding at 0.7005, supported by a 60bp carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) and its position above the SMA20 (0.6941). However, the options market is pricing significant risk with a VRP of +5.1% (IV 11.6% vs HV30 6.5%), the second-highest in the G10 space. This volatility premium reflects the conflict between the RBA's hike bias and a strong negative CESI score of -13, indicating persistent domestic data misses. While AUD is the G10 leader this week (+0.418%), the lack of data support suggests the rally is driven by carry-seeking flows rather than fundamental strength. Watch for the SMA50 at 0.7046 to act as immediate resistance. Risk: A significant miss in upcoming Australian employment data forces a repricing of the RBA hike bias.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4102, exhibiting a significant correlation break against WTI Oil (z = -1.87σ). Historically neutral, the correlation has plunged to -0.923, meaning the pair is ignoring the 1.58% intraday rise in crude prices ($85.7) that would typically support the CAD. This decoupling is driven by the 150bp carry advantage favoring the USD (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and a heavily crowded CAD COT LF net short of -93,515. Retail sentiment is also 71% short, suggesting the market is attempting to fade the USD strength despite the yield advantage. The pair remains below its SMA20 (1.4154), which serves as the immediate technical ceiling. Risk: WTI Oil breaks above $90, re-establishing the CAD's commodity link and forcing a short cover.
